other_observers: Joe Roller; We found this bird.
specific_location: West of Valco Ponds parking lot in Pueblo
description: Joe Roller and I were walking west from Valco Ponds parking, when I noticed a tanager land in a large cottonwood tree in the dead branches on top. I quickly noticed that the tanager didn't have any wing bars, also had dark wings, a small, thick and short bill. Also, the bird was greenish yellow overall. The wings and tail were the darker than the yellowish underparts and greenish yellow head. The size and shape of the bird was typical tanager like. Bigger than the small passerines, like warblers, and smaller than a robin. The dark wings with no wing bars and the small bill eliminated other tanagers.Behaviors: Seen well high up in cottonwood.Call: nonePlumage: adult
